validated_commands.benchmark: uv run py.test tests/test_processing.py tests/test_filters.py --codspeed -k benchmark --override-ini='addopts='

open_prs.title: [Perf Improver] perf: incremental LRA calculation using sorted list + bisect
open_prs.description: LRA calc: O(n log n)/block -> O(log n)/block. test_block_processor_benchmark[lra]: 5.9ms -> 3.7ms (-37%)

optimization_backlog.desc: RunningSum.add() - bypass property setters for direct attribute access
optimization_backlog.notes: Would save 2-3 Python attribute lookups per add() call in _calc_gating hot path

optimization_backlog.desc: _calc_gating when rel_threshold_changed: use np.dot instead of logical_and + fancy indexing
optimization_backlog.notes: rel_threshold_changed is rare, so impact is minimal in typical usage

optimization_backlog.desc: For very long sessions (>10000 blocks), bisect.insort list shift may become bottleneck
optimization_backlog.notes: Could use a sorted container library or a different algorithm (e.g., histogrammed approximation)

optimization_backlog.desc: process_block: use np.einsum or np.dot for combined square+sum computation
optimization_backlog.notes: Intermediate array for np.square is tiny (channels x gate_size), already vectorized

perf_notes: np.quantile is extremely expensive for small arrays due to Python dispatch overhead
pytest-codspeed (simulation mode) benchmarks with --override-ini=addopts= to disable xdist
cProfile shows _calc_lra was 39% of process_block time before optimization
test dataset for benchmark: 1770-2_Comp_RelGateTest_prefiltered_gated.npz at 48000 Hz, ~100 blocks
